signals.reasoning: This live trade remains decisively broken. BLUR has gone from a peak gain of about $1.21 to a current loss near $-1.79, retaining none of peak P&L, and the request flags it urgent. There is no management case for staying in it.
BTC is stable and liquid, but the board remains no-trade and the live veto argues against opening fresh exposure. Neutral is still the right stance.
ETH is slightly soft, funding is positive, and the board is still no-trade. Not enough edge to matter under a veto regime.
SOL is modestly constructive, but positive funding and a no-trade board keep it below action threshold.
DASH has strong momentum and volume, but the board is still neutral and funding is positive. Watchlist only while veto is active.

ZEC is one of the strongest names on the sheet with real liquidity, strong momentum, and negative funding, but the active veto is exactly when extended continuation trades should be downgraded. Strong watchlist long, not a live trade.
ZEN has decent strength, but funding is positive and the board remains neutral. Interesting, not actionable while veto is active.
MERL is moving, but liquidity is thin and the mechanical edge remains neutral. Not a priority name.
PYTH has constructive price action and acceptable liquidity, but funding is positive and the board is still neutral. It stays below trade threshold.
MON has become more balanced and no longer offers a clean bearish asymmetry. It does not stand out enough on either side here.

TNSR still screens as a valid squeeze-continuation long mechanically, but the live geopolitical veto is a direct reason not to open fresh high-beta exposure. Strong watchlist long, not an active trade.
